prompt: AWS allows users to manage their resources using a web based user interface. What is the name of this interface?
explanation:
The AWS Management Console is a web-based graphical user interface that allows users to access and manage AWS services through a browser. It provides a visual way to configure, monitor, and manage resources without writing code.

Other options:
- A. AWS CLI is a command-line tool, not a web-based interface.
- B. AWS API is a programmatic interface used by developers, not a web UI.
- C. AWS SDK is a set of programming libraries, not a web interface.

prompt: Which of the following is an example of horizontal scaling in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Replacing an existing EC2 instance with a larger, more powerful one.
Increasing the compute capacity of a single EC2 instance to address the growing demands of an application.
Adding more RAM capacity to an EC2 instance.
Adding more EC2 instances of the same size to handle an increase in traffic.

explanation:
Horizontal scaling (scaling out) means adding more instances of the same type to distribute the workload. This approach improves fault tolerance and allows handling increased traffic by spreading load across multiple machines.

Other options:
- A. Replacing with a larger instance is vertical scaling (scaling up), not horizontal.
- B. Increasing compute capacity of a single instance is vertical scaling.
- C. Adding more RAM is vertical scaling, as you're enhancing a single instance.

prompt: You have noticed that several critical Amazon EC2 instances have been terminated. Which of the following AWS services would help you determine who took this action?
explanation:
AWS CloudTrail records all API calls made in your AWS account, including who made the call, when it was made, and what actions were taken. It's the go-to service for auditing and tracking user activity.

Other options:
- A. Amazon Inspector assesses security vulnerabilities in applications, not user activity.
- C. AWS Trusted Advisor provides optimization recommendations, not audit logs.
- D. EC2 Instance Usage Report shows resource usage for billing, not who performed actions.

prompt: Which of the below options are related to the reliability of AWS? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Applying the principle of least privilege to all AWS resources.
Automatically provisioning new resources to meet demand.
All AWS services are considered Global Services, and this design helps customers serve their international users.
Providing compensation to customers if issues occur.
Ability to recover quickly from failures.

explanation:
Reliability in AWS refers to the ability of a system to recover from failures and dynamically meet demand. Auto-scaling (B) ensures resources are available when needed, and quick failure recovery (E) minimizes downtime.

Other options:
- A. Least privilege is a security best practice, not a reliability feature.
- C. Not all AWS services are global; most are regional. This relates to global reach, not reliability.
- D. Compensation (SLA credits) is a business practice, not a technical reliability feature.

prompt: Which statement is true regarding the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?

options.text: Responsibilities vary depending on the services used.
Security of the IaaS services is the responsibility of AWS.
Patching the guest OS is always the responsibility of AWS.
Security of the managed services is the responsibility of the customer.

explanation:
The Shared Responsibility Model varies based on the service type. For IaaS (like EC2), customers have more responsibilities, while for managed services (like RDS), AWS handles more. The division of responsibility changes depending on the service.

Other options:
- B. For IaaS services, customers are responsible for securing instances and guest OS.
- C. For IaaS services, patching the guest OS is the customer's responsibility.
- D. For managed services, AWS takes on more security responsibility, not the customer.

prompt: You have set up consolidated billing for several AWS accounts. One of the accounts has purchased a number of reserved instances for 3 years. Which of the following is true regarding this scenario?

options.text: The Reserved Instance discounts can only be shared with the master account.
All accounts can receive the hourly cost benefit of the Reserved Instances.
The purchased instances will have better performance than On-demand instances.
There are no cost benefits from using consolidated billing; It is for informational purposes only.

explanation:
With consolidated billing in AWS Organizations, Reserved Instance discounts are automatically shared across all accounts in the organization. Any account can benefit from the RI pricing when using matching instance types.

Other options:
- A. RI discounts are shared across all member accounts, not just the master account.
- C. Reserved Instances provide cost savings, not performance improvements. Performance is identical to On-Demand.
- D. Consolidated billing provides volume discounts and RI sharing benefits, not just organization.

prompt: A company has developed an eCommerce web application in AWS. What should they do to ensure that the application has the highest level of availability?

options.text: Deploy the application across multiple Availability Zones and Edge locations.
Deploy the application across multiple Availability Zones and subnets.
Deploy the application across multiple Regions and Availability Zones.
Deploy the application across multiple VPC's and subnets.

explanation:
Deploying across multiple Regions and Availability Zones provides the highest level of availability. Multi-Region deployment protects against regional outages, while multi-AZ protects against data center failures within a region.

Other options:
- A. Edge locations are for content delivery (CloudFront), not application hosting.
- B. Multiple subnets within AZs don't provide the same fault tolerance as multi-Region.
- D. Multiple VPCs don't inherently provide availability; geographic distribution does.

prompt: What does AWS Snowball provide? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Built-in computing capabilities that allow customers to process data locally.
A catalog of third-party software solutions that customers need to build solutions and run their businesses.
A hybrid cloud storage between on-premises environments and the AWS Cloud.
An Exabyte-scale data transfer service that allows you to move extremely large amounts of data to AWS.
Secure transfer of large amounts of data into and out of the AWS.

explanation:
AWS Snowball is a petabyte-scale data transport solution that uses secure physical devices. Snowball Edge devices also include computing capabilities for local data processing. It provides secure, encrypted transfer of large data volumes.

Other options:
- B. This describes AWS Marketplace, not Snowball.
- C. This describes AWS Storage Gateway, not Snowball.
- D. Exabyte-scale transfer is AWS Snowmobile, not Snowball. Snowball is petabyte-scale.

prompt: A company has an AWS Enterprise Support plan. They want quick and efficient guidance with their billing and account inquiries. Which of the following should the company use?
explanation:
AWS Support Concierge is a dedicated team available exclusively to Enterprise Support customers that provides expert guidance on billing and account best practices, including assistance with billing inquiries and account management.

Other options:
- A. AWS Health Dashboard shows service health status, not billing support.
- C. AWS Customer Service is available to all customers but lacks the dedicated expertise of Concierge.
- D. AWS Operations Support assists with operational issues, not billing inquiries.

prompt: A Japanese company hosts their applications on Amazon EC2 instances in the Tokyo Region. The company has opened new branches in the United States, and the US users are complaining of high latency. What can the company do to reduce latency for the users in the US while minimizing costs?

options.text: Applying the Amazon Connect latency-based routing policy.
Registering a new US domain name to serve the users in the US.
Building a new data center in the US and implementing a hybrid model.
Deploying new Amazon EC2 instances in a Region located in the US.

explanation:
Deploying EC2 instances in a US Region brings the application closer to US users, reducing network latency significantly. This leverages AWS's global infrastructure without requiring the company to build their own data center.

Other options:
- A. Amazon Connect is a contact center service, not for application latency routing.
- B. A new domain name doesn't reduce physical network latency.
- C. Building a data center is expensive and defeats the purpose of using cloud services.

prompt: An organization has a large number of technical employees who operate their AWS Cloud infrastructure. What does AWS provide to help organize them into teams and then assign the appropriate permissions for each team?
explanation:
IAM user groups allow you to organize users into logical groups and apply permissions to the entire group at once. This simplifies permission management when dealing with teams that need similar access levels.

Other options:
- A. IAM roles are for granting temporary permissions, not for organizing users into teams.
- B. IAM users are individual identities, not a way to organize teams.
- D. AWS Organizations manages multiple AWS accounts, not user groups within an account.

prompt: A company has decided to migrate its Oracle database to AWS. Which AWS service can help achieve this without negatively impacting the functionality of the source database?
explanation:
AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) enables you to migrate databases to AWS with minimal downtime. The source database remains fully operational during migration, reducing impact to applications that depend on it.

Other options:
- A. AWS OpsWorks is for configuration management using Chef/Puppet, not database migration.
- C. AWS Server Migration Service migrates on-premises servers, not specifically databases.
- D. AWS Application Discovery Service helps plan migrations by discovering on-premises infrastructure, but doesn't perform the migration.

prompt: Adjusting compute capacity dynamically to reduce cost is an implementation of which AWS cloud best practice?
explanation:
Elasticity refers to the ability to dynamically scale resources up or down based on demand. This ensures you only pay for the resources you need, reducing costs while maintaining performance during peak times.

Other options:
- A. Security in every layer is about defense-in-depth, not cost optimization through scaling.
- B. Parallelizing tasks improves performance but doesn't relate to dynamic capacity adjustment.
- D. Monolithic architecture is the opposite of cloud best practices and doesn't enable elasticity.

prompt: What are the benefits of having infrastructure hosted in AWS? (Choose TWO)

options.text: There is no need to worry about security.
Gaining complete control over the physical infrastructure.
Operating applications on behalf of customers.
All of the physical security and most of the data/network security are taken care of for you.

explanation:
AWS enables rapid resource provisioning (speed and agility) and handles physical security of data centers along with much of the infrastructure security. This allows customers to focus on their applications rather than managing physical hardware.

Other options:
- B. Security is still a shared responsibility; customers must secure their applications and data.
- C. AWS manages the physical infrastructure; customers don't have physical access.
- D. AWS provides the platform, but customers operate their own applications.

prompt: What is the advantage of the AWS-recommended practice of "decoupling" applications?

options.text: Allows treating an application as a single, cohesive unit.
Reduces inter-dependencies so that failures do not impact other components of the application.
Allows updates of any monolithic application quickly and easily.
Allows tracking of any API call made to any AWS service.

explanation:
Decoupling means designing application components to operate independently. When components are decoupled, a failure in one component doesn't cascade to others, improving overall application resilience and reliability.

Other options:
- A. Treating as a single unit describes tight coupling, the opposite of decoupling.
- C. Monolithic applications are difficult to update; decoupling breaks them into manageable pieces.
- D. API call tracking is done by CloudTrail, not related to decoupling.

prompt: Which of the following helps a customer view the Amazon EC2 billing activity for the past month?
explanation:
AWS Cost & Usage Reports provide the most comprehensive billing data, including detailed information about your AWS costs and usage. They can be configured to show EC2 billing activity and other service costs over time.

Other options:
- A. AWS Budgets helps set spending alerts but isn't designed for detailed historical billing views.
- B. AWS Pricing Calculator estimates future costs, not historical billing activity.
- C. AWS Systems Manager manages operational tasks, not billing information.

prompt: What do you gain from setting up consolidated billing for five different AWS accounts under another master account?

options.text: AWS services' costs will be reduced to half the original price.
The consolidated billing feature is just for organizational purpose.
Each AWS account gets volume discounts.
Each AWS account gets five times the free-tier services capacity.

explanation:
Consolidated billing combines usage from all accounts in an organization to qualify for volume pricing discounts. The aggregated usage may reach pricing tiers faster, resulting in lower overall costs for all accounts.

Other options:
- A. There's no guaranteed 50% reduction; savings depend on usage volume.
- B. Consolidated billing provides real cost benefits through volume discounts and RI sharing.
- D. Free tier limits are per account and don't multiply with consolidated billing.

prompt: What should you do in order to keep the data on EBS volumes safe? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Regularly update firmware on EBS devices.
Ensure that EBS data is encrypted at rest.
Store a backup daily in an external drive.
Prevent any unauthorized access to AWS data centers.

explanation:
EBS snapshots provide point-in-time backups stored in S3, protecting against data loss. Encryption at rest protects data from unauthorized access if the physical storage is compromised. Both are customer responsibilities in the shared model.

Other options:
- A. EBS firmware management is AWS's responsibility, not the customer's.
- D. External drives aren't a cloud best practice; use AWS backup solutions like snapshots.
- E. Physical data center security is AWS's responsibility under the shared responsibility model.

prompt: One of the most important AWS best-practices to follow is the cloud architecture principle of elasticity. How does this principle improve your architecture's design?

options.text: By automatically scaling your on-premises resources based on changes in demand.
By automatically scaling your AWS resources using an Elastic Load Balancer.
By reducing interdependencies between application components wherever possible.
By automatically provisioning the required AWS resources based on changes in demand.

explanation:
Elasticity in AWS means automatically adjusting resource capacity (scaling up or down) based on actual demand. This ensures optimal performance during peak times while minimizing costs during low-demand periods.

Other options:
- A. Elasticity applies to cloud resources, not on-premises infrastructure.
- B. ELB distributes traffic but doesn't provision new resources; Auto Scaling does that.
- C. Reducing interdependencies describes loose coupling, not elasticity.

prompt: A startup company is operating on limited funds and is extremely concerned about cost overruns. Which of the below options can be used to notify the company when their monthly AWS bill exceeds $2000? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Setup a CloudWatch billing alarm that triggers an SNS notification when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure the Amazon Simple Email Service to send billing alerts to their email address on a daily basis.
Configure the AWS Budgets Service to alert the company when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure AWS CloudTrail to automatically delete all AWS resources when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure the Amazon Connect Service to alert the company when the threshold is exceeded.

explanation:
CloudWatch billing alarms monitor your estimated charges and trigger SNS notifications when thresholds are exceeded. AWS Budgets provides more advanced cost tracking and alerts when spending approaches or exceeds defined limits.

Other options:
- B. SES is for sending emails but doesn't have built-in billing monitoring capabilities.
- D. CloudTrail logs API activity but cannot delete resources or monitor billing.
- E. Amazon Connect is a contact center service, not a billing monitoring tool.

prompt: What does Amazon CloudFront use to distribute content to global users with low latency?
explanation:
CloudFront is a CDN that uses Edge Locations—points of presence distributed worldwide—to cache and deliver content closer to end users. This significantly reduces latency by serving content from nearby locations.

Other options:
- A. Global Accelerator optimizes network paths but uses different infrastructure than CloudFront.
- B. Regions contain the main AWS data centers but aren't the edge network CloudFront uses.
- D. Availability Zones are isolated data centers within regions, not edge cache locations.

prompt: What does the "Principle of Least Privilege" refer to?

options.text: You should grant your users only the permissions they need when they need them and nothing more.
All IAM users should have at least the necessary permissions to access the core AWS services.
All trusted IAM users should have access to any AWS service in the respective AWS account.
IAM users should not be granted any permissions; to keep your account safe.

explanation:
The Principle of Least Privilege means granting only the minimum permissions necessary for users to perform their job functions. This reduces the potential impact of compromised credentials or accidental changes.

Other options:
- B. This is too permissive; users should only have access to what they specifically need.
- C. Granting access to any service violates least privilege and increases security risk.
- D. No permissions would prevent users from doing their jobs; they need appropriate access.

prompt: Which of the following does NOT belong to the AWS Cloud Computing models?
explanation:
The three main cloud computing models are IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS. Networking as a Service (NaaS) is not one of the standard cloud computing service models recognized by AWS and the broader industry.

Other options:
- A. PaaS is a valid model (e.g., AWS Elastic Beanstalk).
- B. IaaS is a valid model (e.g., Amazon EC2).
- C. SaaS is a valid model (e.g., AWS Managed Services, Amazon WorkMail).

prompt: The identification process of an online financial services company requires that new users must complete an online interview with their security team. The completed recorded interviews are only required in the event of a legal issue or a regulatory compliance breach. What is the most cost-effective service to store the recorded videos?
explanation:
S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost storage class in AWS, designed for data that is rarely accessed and can tolerate retrieval times of 12+ hours. It's perfect for long-term archival of compliance records.

Other options:
- A. Intelligent-Tiering is more expensive and designed for data with changing access patterns.
- B. AWS Marketplace is for purchasing software, not storage.
- D. EBS is block storage for EC2 instances and is more expensive for archival purposes.

prompt: Which service provides DNS in the AWS cloud?
explanation:
Amazon Route 53 is AWS's highly available and scalable Domain Name System (DNS) web service. It translates domain names to IP addresses and offers domain registration, DNS routing, and health checking.

Other options:
- B. AWS Config tracks resource configurations and compliance, not DNS.
- C. CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN), not a DNS service.
- D. Amazon EMR is for big data processing using Hadoop/Spark, not DNS.

prompt: Hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks are recorded every month worldwide. What service does AWS provide to help protect AWS Customers from these attacks? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS Shield provides protection against DDoS attacks—Shield Standard is free and protects against common attacks, while Shield Advanced offers enhanced protection. AWS WAF filters malicious web traffic and can block attack patterns.

Other options:
- B. AWS Config monitors resource configurations, not DDoS protection.
- C. Amazon Cognito handles user authentication, not DDoS protection.
- E. AWS KMS manages encryption keys, not network-level attack protection.

prompt: A company is deploying a new two-tier web application in AWS. Where should the most frequently accessed data be stored so that the application's response time is optimal?
explanation:
Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory caching service that provides sub-millisecond response times. It's ideal for storing frequently accessed data because memory access is much faster than disk or database queries.

Other options:
- A. AWS OpsWorks is a configuration management service, not a data storage solution.
- B. Storage Gateway connects on-premises storage to cloud, not for caching frequently accessed data.
- C. EBS volumes are block storage and slower than in-memory caching for frequent access patterns.

prompt: You want to run a questionnaire application for only one day (without interruption), which Amazon EC2 purchase option should you use?
explanation:
On-Demand instances are ideal for short-term, unpredictable workloads that cannot be interrupted. They have no long-term commitment and are charged by the hour or second, perfect for a one-day application.

Other options:
- A. Reserved Instances require 1-3 year commitments and aren't cost-effective for one day.
- B. Spot Instances can be terminated by AWS with little notice, risking interruption.
- C. Dedicated Instances are for compliance needs and are more expensive than necessary.

prompt: You are working on a project that involves creating thumbnails of millions of images. Consistent uptime is not an issue, and continuous processing is not required. Which EC2 buying option would be the most cost-effective?
explanation:
Spot Instances offer up to 90% discount compared to On-Demand pricing. Since the workload can tolerate interruptions and doesn't require continuous processing, Spot Instances provide the best cost savings.

Other options:
- A. Reserved Instances require long-term commitment and aren't optimal for variable workloads.
- B. On-Demand is more expensive than Spot for interruptible workloads.
- C. Dedicated Instances are the most expensive option and unnecessary for this use case.

prompt: Which of the following can be described as a global content delivery network (CDN) service?
explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is AWS's global CDN service that delivers content (web pages, videos, APIs) through a worldwide network of edge locations, reducing latency by caching content closer to users.

Other options:
- A. AWS VPN provides secure network connections, not content delivery.
- B. Direct Connect is a dedicated network connection to AWS, not a CDN.
- C. Regions are geographic areas containing AWS data centers, not a CDN service.

prompt: Which of the following services allows customers to manage their agreements with AWS?
explanation:
AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports and allows customers to review, accept, and manage agreements with AWS, such as the Business Associate Addendum (BAA) for HIPAA compliance.

Other options:
- B. Certificate Manager handles SSL/TLS certificates, not AWS agreements.
- C. Systems Manager manages operational tasks on AWS resources, not agreements.
- D. Organizations manages multiple AWS accounts, not compliance agreements.

prompt: Which of the following are examples of AWS-Managed Services, where AWS is responsible for the operational and maintenance burdens of running the service? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Amazon DynamoDB and Amazon EMR are fully managed services where AWS handles infrastructure, patching, scaling, and maintenance. Customers focus on using the service rather than managing the underlying infrastructure.

Other options:
- A. VPC is a networking service that customers configure and manage themselves.
- D. IAM is a free service for access management, not a managed compute/database service.
- E. EC2 requires customers to manage the operating system, patching, and scaling.

prompt: Your company has a data store application that requires access to a NoSQL database. Which AWS database offering would meet this requirement?
explanation:
Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's fully managed NoSQL database service. It provides fast, flexible, and scalable key-value and document database capabilities with single-digit millisecond performance.

Other options:
- A. Aurora is a relational database (SQL), not NoSQL.
- C. EBS is block storage for EC2 instances, not a database service.
- D. Redshift is a relational data warehouse for analytics, not a NoSQL database.

prompt: As part of the Enterprise support plan, who is the primary point of contact for ongoing support needs?

options.text: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user.

explanation:
The Technical Account Manager (TAM) is a dedicated resource assigned to Enterprise Support customers. They provide proactive guidance, coordinate access to AWS experts, and serve as the primary point of contact for support needs.

Other options:
- A. IAM users are identities in your account, not AWS support personnel.
- B. IEM engineers assist with specific events like launches, not ongoing support.
- C. Consulting Partners are external companies, not part of AWS Support.

prompt: How can you view the distribution of AWS spending in one of your AWS accounts?

options.text: By contacting the AWS Support team.
By contacting the AWS Finance team.

explanation:
AWS Cost Explorer is a tool that provides visualization and analysis of your AWS costs and usage. It allows you to view spending distribution by service, account, tags, and other dimensions.

Other options:
- A. VPC console manages networking resources, not cost information.
- B. Contacting support is unnecessary; Cost Explorer provides self-service access.
- D. There's no need to contact AWS Finance; cost data is available in the console.

prompt: Which of the following must an IAM user provide to interact with AWS services using the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI)?
explanation:
AWS CLI requires Access Keys (consisting of an Access Key ID and Secret Access Key) to authenticate programmatic requests. These credentials identify the IAM user and allow secure API calls to AWS services.

Other options:
- B. "Secret token" is not a valid AWS credential type for CLI access.
- C. UserID alone cannot authenticate requests; access keys are required.
- D. Username and password are for console login, not CLI authentication.

prompt: You have AWS Basic support, and you have discovered that some AWS resources are being used maliciously, and those resources could potentially compromise your data. What should you do?

options.text: Contact the AWS Customer Service team.

explanation:
The AWS Abuse team handles reports of AWS resources being used for malicious purposes, including spam, DDoS attacks, or other abusive activities. They investigate and take action against abusive behavior.

Other options:
- A. Customer Service handles general inquiries, not abuse reports.
- C. The Concierge team is only available to Enterprise Support customers for billing guidance.
- D. While security-related, the specific team for abuse reports is the AWS Abuse team.

prompt: Select TWO examples of the AWS shared controls.
explanation:
Shared controls apply to both AWS and customers but in different contexts. Patch Management is shared—AWS patches infrastructure, customers patch their guest OS. Configuration Management is shared—AWS configures infrastructure, customers configure their applications.

Other options:
- B. IAM Management is solely the customer's responsibility.
- C. VPC Management is primarily the customer's responsibility.
- E. Data Center operations are entirely AWS's responsibility.

prompt: In order to implement best practices when dealing with a "Single Point of Failure," you should attempt to build as much automation as possible in both detecting and reacting to failure. Which of the following AWS services would help? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) automatically detects unhealthy instances and routes traffic to healthy ones. Auto Scaling automatically replaces failed instances and adjusts capacity, both helping eliminate single points of failure.

Other options:
- C. Amazon Athena (misspelled) is for querying data in S3, not fault tolerance.
- D. ECR (Elastic Container Registry) stores container images, not for failure handling.
- E. EC2 alone doesn't automatically detect or recover from failures without Auto Scaling.

prompt: A company is planning to host an educational website on AWS. Their video courses will be streamed all around the world. Which of the following AWS services will help achieve high transfer speeds?
explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is a CDN that caches video content at edge locations worldwide. This enables fast streaming by serving content from locations geographically close to viewers, reducing latency and improving transfer speeds.

Other options:
- A. SNS is a notification service for sending messages, not video streaming.
- B. Kinesis Video Streams is for capturing and processing video streams, not CDN delivery.
- C. CloudFormation is for infrastructure as code, not content delivery.

prompt: A developer is planning to build a two-tier web application that has a MySQL database layer. Which of the following AWS database services would provide automated backups for the application?

options.text: A MySQL database installed on an EC2 instance.

explanation:
Amazon Aurora is a MySQL-compatible relational database that provides automated backups, point-in-time recovery, and continuous backup to S3. It's a fully managed service that handles backup operations automatically.

Other options:
- A. Self-managed MySQL on EC2 requires you to configure and manage your own backups.
- C. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, not MySQL-compatible.
- D. Neptune is a graph database, not a MySQL-compatible database.

prompt: What is the AWS service that enables AWS architects to manage infrastructure as code?
explanation:
AWS CloudFormation allows you to define infrastructure using JSON or YAML templates. This infrastructure as code approach enables version control, repeatable deployments, and automated provisioning of AWS resources.

Other options:
- B. AWS Config monitors and records resource configurations but doesn't create infrastructure.
- C. Amazon SES is an email sending service, not for infrastructure management.
- D. Amazon EMR is for big data processing with Hadoop/Spark, not infrastructure as code.

prompt: Under the shared responsibility model, which of the following is the responsibility of AWS?
explanation:
AWS is responsible for "security of the cloud," which includes configuring and managing infrastructure devices like routers, switches, and physical servers. Customers don't have access to configure underlying infrastructure hardware.

Other options:
- A. Client-side encryption is the customer's responsibility to implement.
- C. Server-side encryption configuration is the customer's choice to enable.
- D. Security Groups are configured by customers to control their traffic.

prompt: What does the AWS Health Dashboard provide? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Detailed troubleshooting guidance to address AWS events impacting your resources.
Health checks for Auto Scaling instances.
A dashboard detailing vulnerabilities in your applications.
Personalized view of AWS service health.

explanation:
AWS Health Dashboard provides a personalized view of service health affecting your specific resources and offers detailed troubleshooting guidance for events impacting your account. It shows both account-specific and general AWS service health.

Other options:
- B. Auto Scaling health checks are managed through EC2/Auto Scaling, not Health Dashboard.
- C. Cost optimization recommendations come from AWS Trusted Advisor, not Health Dashboard.
- D. Application vulnerability scanning is done by Amazon Inspector, not Health Dashboard.

prompt: You have deployed your application on multiple Amazon EC2 instances. Your customers complain that sometimes they can't reach your application. Which AWS service allows you to monitor the performance of your EC2 instances to assist in troubleshooting these issues?
explanation:
Amazon CloudWatch monitors AWS resources and applications in real-time. It collects metrics like CPU utilization, network traffic, and can set alarms to alert you when performance issues occur, helping troubleshoot availability problems.

Other options:
- A. Lambda is a serverless compute service, not a monitoring tool.
- B. AWS Config tracks configuration changes, not real-time performance metrics.
- D. CloudTrail logs API activity, not EC2 performance metrics.

prompt: Your company is developing a critical web application in AWS, and the security of the application is a top priority. Which of the following AWS services will provide infrastructure security optimization recommendations?
explanation:
AWS Trusted Advisor provides recommendations across five categories including Security. It analyzes your AWS environment and suggests improvements for security best practices like open security groups, IAM issues, and MFA settings.

Other options:
- A. AWS Shield protects against DDoS but doesn't provide optimization recommendations.
- B. Management Console is the web interface; it doesn't provide security recommendations.
- C. Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets but doesn't analyze your infrastructure security.

prompt: Which of the following is not a benefit of Amazon S3? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Amazon S3 provides unlimited storage for any type of data.
Amazon S3 can run any type of application or backend system.
Amazon S3 stores any number of objects, but with object size limits.
Amazon S3 can be scaled manually to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere.
Amazon S3 provides 99.999999999% (11 9's) of data durability.

explanation:
S3 is object storage, not a compute service—it cannot run applications or backend systems. S3 scales automatically, not manually. These statements are incorrect about S3's capabilities.

Other options:
- A. S3 does provide virtually unlimited storage capacity.
- C. S3 can store unlimited objects, though individual objects have a 5TB size limit.
- E. S3 does provide 99.999999999% durability for stored objects.

prompt: In the AWS Shared responsibility Model, which of the following are the responsibility of the customer? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Controlling physical access to compute resources.

explanation:
Customers are responsible for "security in the cloud." This includes setting password policies in IAM and configuring Security Groups and NACLs to control network access to their resources.

Other options:
- A. Disk disposal is AWS's responsibility in managing physical hardware.
- B. Physical access control to data centers is AWS's responsibility.
- C. Network infrastructure patching is AWS's responsibility.

prompt: What does AWS provide to deploy popular technologies such as IBM MQ on AWS with the least amount of effort and time?
explanation:
AWS Quick Starts are automated reference deployments built by AWS architects and partners. They use CloudFormation templates to deploy popular technologies quickly, following AWS best practices with minimal effort.

Other options:
- A. Aurora is a database service, not for deploying third-party software like IBM MQ.
- B. CloudWatch monitors resources but doesn't deploy software.
- D. OpsWorks is for configuration management, not pre-built deployments.

prompt: An organization has decided to purchase an Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance (RI) for three years in order to reduce costs. It is possible that the application workloads could change during the reservation period. What is the EC2 Reserved Instance (RI) type that will allow the company to exchange the purchased reserved instance for another reserved instance with higher computing power if they need to?
explanation:
Convertible Reserved Instances allow you to exchange your RI for another RI with different instance attributes, including instance family, OS, and tenancy. This provides flexibility when workload requirements change during the reservation period.

Other options:
- A. "Elastic RI" is not a valid Reserved Instance type.
- B. "Premium RI" is not a valid Reserved Instance type.
- C. Standard RIs offer higher discounts but cannot be exchanged for different instance types.

prompt: A global company with a large number of AWS accounts is seeking a way in which they can centrally manage billing and security policies across all accounts. Which AWS Service will assist them in meeting these goals?
explanation:
AWS Organizations enables centralized management of multiple AWS accounts. It provides consolidated billing to combine payments across accounts and Service Control Policies (SCPs) to centrally manage security policies and permissions.

Other options:
- B. Trusted Advisor provides recommendations but doesn't manage multiple accounts or billing.
- C. IAM User Groups manage users within a single account, not across multiple accounts.
- D. AWS Config monitors resource configurations but doesn't provide centralized billing or policy management.

prompt: Which service provides object-level storage in AWS?
explanation:
Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service) is AWS's object storage service. It stores data as objects with metadata in buckets, allowing you to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere on the web.

Other options:
- A. Amazon EBS provides block-level storage for EC2 instances, not object storage.
- B. Instance Store provides temporary block-level storage attached directly to the host.
- C. Amazon EFS is a file storage service using the NFS protocol, not object storage.

prompt: A company is concerned that they are spending money on underutilized compute resources in AWS. Which AWS feature will help ensure that their applications are automatically adding/removing EC2 compute capacity to closely match the required demand?
explanation:
AWS Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances based on demand. It scales out when demand increases and scales in when demand decreases, ensuring you only pay for the resources you actually need.

Other options:
- A. Elastic Load Balancer distributes traffic but doesn't add or remove instances.
- B. AWS Budgets tracks spending and sets alerts but doesn't adjust compute capacity.
- D. Cost Explorer analyzes costs but doesn't automatically manage resources.

prompt: Which S3 storage class is best for data with unpredictable access patterns?
explanation:
S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves data between access tiers based on changing access patterns. It monitors access patterns and moves objects that haven't been accessed to lower-cost tiers, optimizing costs without performance impact.

Other options:
- B. Glacier is for archival data with known infrequent access, not unpredictable patterns.
- C. S3 Standard is cost-effective for frequently accessed data, not unpredictable access.
- D. Standard-IA is for data accessed less frequently but requires rapid access when needed.

prompt: What is the AWS database service that allows you to upload data structured in key-value format?
explanation:
Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that supports key-value and document data structures. It provides single-digit millisecond performance at any scale for applications needing consistent, low-latency data access.

Other options:
- B. Aurora is a relational database (SQL), not designed for key-value data structures.
- C. Redshift is a data warehouse for analytics, using relational (SQL) structure.
- D. RDS provides managed relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, etc.), not key-value stores.

prompt: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding Amazon EC2 On-demand instances?

options.text: You have to pay a start-up fee when launching a new instance for the first time.
The on-demand instances follow the AWS pay-as-you-go pricing model.
With on-demand instances, no longer-term commitments or upfront payments are needed.
When using on-demand Linux instances, you are charged per second based on an hourly rate.

explanation:
There is NO start-up fee for EC2 On-Demand instances. You simply pay for compute capacity by the hour or second with no upfront costs, no minimum commitments, and no long-term contracts.

Other options:
- B. On-Demand does follow pay-as-you-go pricing—you pay only for what you use.
- C. On-Demand requires no commitments or upfront payments; this is correct.
- D. Linux instances are indeed billed per second with a minimum of 60 seconds.

prompt: A company has moved to AWS recently. Which of the following AWS Services will help ensure that they have the proper security settings? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS Trusted Advisor provides security checks including open ports, IAM usage, and MFA on root accounts. Amazon Inspector automatically assesses applications for vulnerabilities and deviations from security best practices.

Other options:
- C. Amazon SNS is a notification service for sending messages, not security assessments.
- D. CloudWatch monitors performance metrics, not security configurations specifically.
- E. Concierge Support assists with billing questions, not security configuration review.

prompt: What is the AWS feature that provides an additional level of security above the default authentication mechanism of usernames and passwords?
explanation:
AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection by requiring a second authentication factor (like a code from a hardware or virtual device) in addition to username and password.

Other options:
- A. Encrypted keys are for data protection, not authentication enhancement.
- B. Email verification is not a standard AWS authentication mechanism.
- C. AWS KMS manages encryption keys, not user authentication.

prompt: A company is introducing a new product to their customers, and is expecting a surge in traffic to their web application. As part of their Enterprise Support plan, which of the following provides the company with architectural and scaling guidance?
explanation:
Infrastructure Event Management (IEM) is an Enterprise Support feature that provides architectural and operational guidance for product launches, migrations, and other planned events. AWS experts help prepare your infrastructure for significant traffic events.

Other options:
- A. Knowledge Center provides self-service documentation, not personalized guidance.
- B. Health Dashboard shows service status, not architectural planning support.
- D. Support Concierge assists with billing and account questions, not architectural guidance.

prompt: You work as an on-premises MySQL DBA. The work of database configuration, backups, patching, and DR can be time-consuming and repetitive. Your company has decided to migrate to the AWS Cloud. Which of the following can help save time on database maintenance so you can focus on data architecture and performance?
explanation:
Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service that handles routine database tasks like provisioning, patching, backup, recovery, and scaling. It supports MySQL and lets DBAs focus on optimization rather than administration.

Other options:
- B. Redshift is a data warehouse, not a MySQL replacement for transactional databases.
- C. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, not compatible with MySQL workloads.
- D. CloudWatch is for monitoring, not database management.

prompt: Which of the below is a best-practice when designing solutions on AWS?

options.text: Invest heavily in architecting your environment, as it is not easy to change your design later.
Use AWS reservations to reduce costs when testing your production environment.
Automate wherever possible to make architectural experimentation easier.
Provision a large compute capacity to handle any spikes in load

explanation:
Automation is a core AWS best practice. Infrastructure as Code and automation enable rapid experimentation, consistent deployments, and the ability to quickly iterate on architectural designs without manual intervention.

Other options:
- A. AWS makes it easy to change designs; you shouldn't over-invest upfront in rigid architectures.
- B. Reservations require commitment; they're not suitable for testing environments.
- D. Over-provisioning wastes money; use Auto Scaling instead to handle load variations.

prompt: According to the AWS Acceptable Use Policy, which of the following statements is true regarding penetration testing of EC2 instances?

options.text: Penetration testing is not allowed in AWS.
Penetration testing is performed automatically by AWS to determine vulnerabilities in your AWS infrastructure.
Penetration testing can be performed by the customer on their own instances without prior authorization from AWS.
The AWS customers are only allowed to perform penetration testing on services managed by AWS.

explanation:
AWS allows customers to conduct security assessments and penetration testing against their AWS infrastructure without prior approval for several services including EC2, RDS, Aurora, and others. This policy change removed the requirement for pre-authorization.

Other options:
- A. Penetration testing is allowed on customer-owned resources.
- B. AWS doesn't automatically perform penetration testing on customer resources.
- D. Customers test their own resources, not AWS-managed services infrastructure.

prompt: Which service is used to ensure that messages between software components are not lost if one or more components fail?
explanation:
Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables decoupling of application components. Messages are stored in the queue until they're processed, ensuring no messages are lost even if components fail.

Other options:
- B. Amazon SES is an email sending service, not for application messaging.
- C. AWS Direct Connect is a dedicated network connection, not a messaging service.
- D. Amazon Connect is a contact center service for customer communications.

prompt: The principle "design for failure and nothing will fail" is very important when designing your AWS Cloud architecture. Which of the following would help adhere to this principle? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Availability Zones provide isolated locations within a region, protecting against single data center failures. Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic across multiple instances and automatically routes around failed instances, ensuring high availability.

Other options:
- A. MFA is for authentication security, not fault tolerance.
- D. Penetration testing identifies vulnerabilities but doesn't provide fault tolerance.
- E. Vertical scaling upgrades single instances but doesn't address failure scenarios.

prompt: What is the AWS service that provides a virtual network dedicated to your AWS account?
explanation:
Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) lets you provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch resources in a virtual network that you define. You have complete control over your networking environment.

Other options:
- A. AWS VPN provides secure connections to a VPC but isn't the virtual network itself.
- B. Subnets are divisions within a VPC, not the virtual network itself.
- C. Dedicated Hosts are physical servers, not virtual networks.

prompt: According to the AWS Shared responsibility model, which of the following are the responsibility of the customer? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Managing environmental events of AWS data centers.
Protecting the confidentiality of data in transit in Amazon S3.
Controlling physical access to AWS Regions.
Ensuring that the underlying EC2 host is configured properly.
Patching applications installed on Amazon EC2.

explanation:
Customers are responsible for protecting their data (including encryption in transit) and maintaining their applications. This includes implementing SSL/TLS for S3 data transfers and patching any software they install on EC2 instances.

Other options:
- A. Environmental events at data centers are managed by AWS.
- C. Physical access control to AWS facilities is AWS's responsibility.
- D. The underlying EC2 host configuration is managed by AWS.

prompt: Which of the following AWS services can be used as a compute resource? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers for running applications. AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code in response to events. Both are compute services that process workloads.

Other options:
- A. VPC is a networking service, not compute.
- B. CloudWatch is a monitoring service, not compute.
- C. S3 is object storage, not compute.

prompt: Your company is designing a new application that will store and retrieve photos and videos. Which of the following services should you recommend as the underlying storage mechanism?
explanation:
Amazon S3 is ideal for storing media files like photos and videos. It provides virtually unlimited storage, high durability (99.999999999%), and easy retrieval via URLs. It's the standard choice for media storage applications.

Other options:
- A. EBS is block storage for EC2 instances, not ideal for media file distribution.
- B. SQS is a message queue service, not a storage service.
- D. Instance store is temporary storage that's lost when the instance stops.

prompt: Which of the following is equivalent to a user name and password and is used to authenticate your programmatic access to AWS services and APIs?
explanation:
Access Keys consist of an Access Key ID and Secret Access Key, functioning like a username and password for programmatic access. They authenticate CLI commands, API calls, and SDK requests to AWS services.

Other options:
- A. "Instance Password" is not a valid AWS credential type.
- B. Key pairs are for SSH access to EC2 instances, not API authentication.
- D. MFA is an additional security factor, not a replacement for basic credentials.

prompt: What does Amazon ElastiCache provide?

options.text: An Ehcache compatible in-memory data store.
An online software store that allows Customers to launch pre-configured software with just few clicks.
A domain name system in the cloud.

explanation:
Amazon ElastiCache is a fully managed in-memory caching service supporting Redis and Memcached. It significantly improves application performance by retrieving data from fast, in-memory caches instead of slower disk-based databases.

Other options:
- B. ElastiCache supports Redis and Memcached, not Ehcache specifically.
- C. This describes AWS Marketplace, not ElastiCache.
- D. This describes Route 53, not ElastiCache.

prompt: What is the AWS service that enables you to manage all of your AWS accounts from a single master account?
explanation:
AWS Organizations allows you to centrally manage and govern multiple AWS accounts. From a management account, you can create accounts, organize them into OUs, apply policies, and consolidate billing.

Other options:
- A. AWS WAF is a web application firewall, not for account management.
- B. Trusted Advisor provides recommendations, not multi-account management.
- D. AWS Config (not Amazon Config) monitors resource configurations, not accounts.

prompt: Which of the following EC2 instance purchasing options supports the Bring Your Own License (BYOL) model for almost every BYOL scenario?
explanation:
Dedicated Hosts provide visibility into the physical server, including sockets and cores, which is required for most BYOL licensing agreements. They give you full control over instance placement on a dedicated physical server.

Other options:
- A. Dedicated Instances run on dedicated hardware but don't provide the server visibility needed for BYOL.
- C. On-demand instances run on shared hardware, not suitable for most BYOL scenarios.
- D. Reserved Instances are a pricing model, not a hosting type for BYOL.

prompt: Which of the following is one of the benefits of moving infrastructure from an on-premises data center to AWS?

options.text: Free support for all enterprise customers.
AWS holds responsibility for managing customer applications.

explanation:
Moving to AWS converts capital expenditure (buying hardware) to operational expenditure (paying for usage). This eliminates upfront investments in servers, data centers, and infrastructure, converting to a pay-as-you-go model.

Other options:
- A. Enterprise support is a paid service, not free.
- B. Data protection requires customer configuration; it's not fully automatic.
- D. Customers manage their own applications; AWS manages infrastructure.

prompt: Which of the following are important design principles you should adopt when designing systems on AWS? (Choose TWO)

options.text: Always use Global Services in your architecture rather than Regional Services.
Always choose to pay as you go.

explanation:
Automation enables consistent, repeatable deployments and reduces human error. Removing single points of failure ensures high availability—if one component fails, the system continues to operate through redundancy.

Other options:
- A. Use the appropriate service type; regional services often provide better performance and compliance.
- B. Pay-as-you-go is a pricing model, not a design principle for architecture.
- C. Servers should be treated as disposable resources, not fixed—this enables automation and recovery.

prompt: Which AWS Service can be used to establish a dedicated, private network connection between AWS and your datacenter?
explanation:
AWS Direct Connect establishes a dedicated, private network connection from your premises to AWS. This provides more consistent network performance, reduced bandwidth costs, and a more secure connection than internet-based connections.

Other options:
- B. CloudFront is a CDN for content delivery, not datacenter connectivity.
- C. Snowball is for physical data transfer via devices, not network connectivity.
- D. Route 53 is a DNS service, not for establishing network connections.

prompt: You are working on two projects that require completely different network configurations. Which AWS service or feature will allow you to isolate resources and network configurations?
explanation:
Amazon VPC allows you to create isolated virtual networks for different projects. Each VPC has its own IP range, subnets, routing tables, and security settings, providing complete network isolation between projects.

Other options:
- A. Internet gateways connect VPCs to the internet but don't isolate resources.
- C. Security Groups filter traffic but don't provide complete network isolation.
- D. CloudFront is a CDN, not for network isolation.

prompt: Which of the following services can help protect your web applications from SQL injection and other vulnerabilities in your application code?
explanation:
AWS Web Application Firewall (WAF) protects web applications from common exploits like SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and other OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities. It filters malicious requests before they reach your application.

Other options:
- A. Cognito handles user authentication, not application vulnerability protection.
- B. IAM manages access permissions, not application-layer attacks.
- C. Aurora is a database service that could be a target of SQL injection, not protection against it.

prompt: An organization needs to analyze and process a large number of data sets. Which AWS service should they use?
explanation:
Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) is a cloud big data platform for processing vast amounts of data using open-source tools like Apache Hadoop, Spark, and Hive. It's designed for large-scale data processing and analysis.

Other options:
- B. Amazon MQ is a message broker service, not for data analysis.
- C. Amazon SNS is a notification service for pub/sub messaging.
- D. Amazon SQS is a message queue service, not for data processing.

prompt: Based on the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which of the following are the sole responsibility of AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS is responsible for "security of the cloud"—the infrastructure that runs AWS services. This includes creating and managing hypervisors and all hardware maintenance in AWS data centers.

Other options:
- A. Network monitoring can be a shared responsibility; customers monitor their applications.
- B. Installing software on EC2 is the customer's responsibility.
- D. Configuring ACLs (Network ACLs) is the customer's responsibility.

prompt: What is the AWS service that provides you the highest level of control over the underlying virtual infrastructure?
explanation:
Amazon EC2 provides Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) with the highest level of control. You choose the OS, instance type, storage, networking, and have root/administrator access to configure the virtual server as needed.

Other options:
- A. Redshift is a managed data warehouse; AWS manages the underlying infrastructure.
- B. DynamoDB is fully managed; you have no control over underlying servers.
- D. RDS is managed; AWS handles OS patching, backups, and database maintenance.

prompt: What are the default security credentials that are required to access the AWS management console for an IAM user account?
explanation:
IAM users access the AWS Management Console using a username and password. These are the default credentials for console-based (web browser) access to AWS services.

Other options:
- A. MFA is an additional security layer, not the default credentials.
- B. Security tokens are for temporary credentials, not default console access.
- D. Access keys are for programmatic (CLI/API) access, not console login.

prompt: In your on-premises environment, you can create as many virtual servers as you need from a single template. What can you use to perform the same in AWS?
explanation:
An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) is a template that contains the software configuration (OS, application server, applications) needed to launch an EC2 instance. You can launch multiple identical instances from a single AMI.

Other options:
- A. IAM manages access and permissions, not server templates.
- B. Internet gateway enables VPC internet connectivity, not server creation.
- C. EBS Snapshots backup volumes but aren't complete server templates.

prompt: What are two advantages of using Cloud Computing over using traditional data centers? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Cloud computing enables distributed infrastructure across multiple regions and availability zones, unlike single data centers. This geographic distribution helps eliminate single points of failure, improving availability and disaster recovery.

Other options:
- A. Reserved capacity exists in both cloud and traditional data centers.
- D. Virtualization exists in traditional data centers too.
- E. Dedicated hosting is available in both models and isn't a cloud-specific advantage.

prompt: Which of the following aspects of security are managed by AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS manages the physical infrastructure security including hardware patching and securing data centers, network infrastructure, and the physical facilities globally. These are "security of the cloud" responsibilities.

Other options:
- A. Customers choose to enable and configure EBS encryption.
- B. VPC security (Security Groups, NACLs) is configured by customers.
- C. Access permissions (IAM policies) are managed by customers.

prompt: Which statement best describes the operational excellence pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?

options.text: The ability of a system to recover gracefully from failure.
The efficient use of computing resources to meet requirements.
The ability to monitor systems and improve supporting processes and procedures.
The ability to manage datacenter operations more efficiently.

explanation:
Operational Excellence focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value and continually improve processes. It includes automating changes, responding to events, and defining standards for daily operations.

Other options:
- A. This describes the Reliability pillar.
- B. This describes the Performance Efficiency pillar.
- D. Managing datacenters is AWS's responsibility; this isn't an operational excellence focus.

prompt: AWS has created a large number of Edge Locations as part of its Global Infrastructure. Which of the following is NOT a benefit of using Edge Locations?

options.text: Edge locations are used by CloudFront to cache the most recent responses.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to improve your end users' experience when uploading files.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to distribute traffic across multiple instances to reduce latency.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to distribute content to global users with low latency.

explanation:
Edge Locations do NOT distribute traffic across EC2 instances—that's what Elastic Load Balancers do. Edge Locations cache content closer to users; they're not involved in load balancing between application instances.

Other options:
- A. CloudFront does cache responses at Edge Locations.
- B. S3 Transfer Acceleration uses Edge Locations to speed up uploads.
- D. Content delivery with low latency is the primary purpose of Edge Locations.

prompt: What are the change management tools that helps AWS customers audit and monitor all resource changes in their AWS environment? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS CloudTrail records API calls and user activity for auditing. AWS Config continuously monitors and records resource configurations, allowing you to track changes over time and evaluate compliance against desired configurations.

Other options:
- B. Amazon Comprehend is a natural language processing service.
- C. Transit Gateway connects VPCs and on-premises networks, not for change monitoring.
- D. X-Ray traces application requests for debugging, not infrastructure changes.

prompt: Which of the following services allows you to run containerized applications on a cluster of EC2 instances?
explanation:
Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) is a fully managed container orchestration service that allows you to run Docker containers on a cluster of EC2 instances. It handles scheduling, scaling, and managing containers.

Other options:
- B. Data Pipeline orchestrates data workflows, not containers.
- C. Cloud9 is a cloud-based IDE, not a container orchestration service.
- D. Personal Health Dashboard shows AWS service health affecting your resources.

prompt: Which of the following services will help businesses ensure compliance in AWS?
explanation:
AWS CloudTrail provides governance, compliance, and audit capabilities by recording all API calls made in your AWS account. This audit trail is essential for compliance requirements, security analysis, and troubleshooting.

Other options:
- A. CloudFront is a CDN, not for compliance monitoring.
- B. CloudEndure Migration is for server migration, not compliance.
- C. CloudWatch monitors performance metrics, not specifically for compliance auditing.

prompt: Which of the following procedures will help reduce your Amazon S3 costs?

options.text: Use the Import/Export feature to move old files automatically to Amazon Glacier.
Use the right combination of storage classes based on different use cases.
Pick the right Availability Zone for your S3 bucket.
Move all the data stored in S3 standard to EBS.

explanation:
Using appropriate S3 storage classes (Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, Glacier, etc.) based on access patterns significantly reduces costs. Lifecycle policies can automatically transition objects to cheaper tiers over time.

Other options:
- A. Import/Export is deprecated; use S3 Lifecycle policies instead.
- C. S3 buckets are regional, not AZ-specific; you don't choose AZs for buckets.
- D. Moving to EBS would likely increase costs and isn't appropriate for object storage use cases.

prompt: What are the AWS services/features that can help you maintain a highly available and fault-tolerant architecture in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
EC2 Auto Scaling automatically maintains application availability by adjusting capacity and replacing unhealthy instances. Elastic Load Balancer distributes traffic across multiple instances and automatically routes around failures.

Other options:
- A. Direct Connect provides dedicated connectivity but isn't for high availability within AWS.
- D. CloudFormation automates infrastructure deployment but doesn't provide runtime high availability.
- E. Network ACLs filter traffic but don't contribute to high availability or fault tolerance.

prompt: Which of the following activities may help reduce your AWS monthly costs?

options.text: Enabling Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling for all of your workloads.
Using the AWS Network Load Balancer (NLB) to load balance the incoming HTTP requests.
Removing all of your Cost Allocation Tags.
Deploying your AWS resources across multiple Availability Zones.

explanation:
EC2 Auto Scaling helps reduce costs by automatically scaling down resources when demand decreases. You avoid paying for idle capacity by matching resource provisioning to actual workload requirements.

Other options:
- B. NLB is for TCP/UDP traffic; Application Load Balancer is for HTTP. Neither directly reduces costs.
- C. Removing tags makes cost tracking harder but doesn't reduce actual costs.
- D. Multi-AZ deployment improves availability but typically increases costs.

prompt: What is the AWS service/feature that takes advantage of Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations to transfer files to S3 with higher upload speeds?
explanation:
S3 Transfer Acceleration uses CloudFront's edge locations to speed up transfers to S3. Data is uploaded to the nearest edge location and then transferred to S3 over AWS's optimized network backbone.

Other options:
- B. AWS WAF is a web application firewall, not for file transfers.
- C. Snowmobile is a physical truck for exabyte-scale data transfer.
- D. Snowball is a physical device for large data transfers, not using edge locations.

prompt: Which of the following AWS security features is associated with an EC2 instance and functions to filter incoming traffic requests?
explanation:
Security Groups are virtual firewalls associated with EC2 instances that control inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level. They are stateful and filter traffic based on rules you define.

Other options:
- A. X-Ray is for application tracing, not traffic filtering.
- B. Network ACLs operate at the subnet level, not instance level.
- D. VPC Flow Logs capture traffic information but don't filter it.

prompt: Which AWS services can be used to improve the performance of a global application and reduce latency for its users? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
AWS Global Accelerator improves global application performance by routing traffic through AWS's global network to the optimal endpoint. CloudFront caches content at edge locations worldwide, reducing latency for content delivery.

Other options:
- A. KMS is for encryption key management, not performance optimization.
- C. Direct Connect is for dedicated connectivity, not global performance improvement.
- D. AWS Glue is an ETL service for data preparation, not for application performance.

prompt: Using Amazon RDS falls under the shared responsibility model. Which of the following are customer responsibilities? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
With RDS, customers are responsible for designing their database schema (tables, relationships) and managing database settings like parameters, users, and permissions. AWS handles the underlying infrastructure and database engine maintenance.

Other options:
- B. AWS handles automated backups for RDS.
- D. AWS patches the database software for RDS.
- E. AWS installs and manages the database software for RDS.

prompt: A company has a large amount of structured data stored in their on-premises data center. They are planning to migrate all the data to AWS, what is the most appropriate AWS database option?
explanation:
Amazon RDS is ideal for structured data that typically resides in relational databases. It supports popular engines like MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, and SQL Server, making migration from on-premises relational databases straightforward.

Other options:
- A. DynamoDB is for NoSQL/unstructured data, not structured relational data.
- B. SNS is a notification service, not a database.
- D. ElastiCache is for caching, not primary data storage.

prompt: A company has created a solution that helps AWS customers improve their architectures on AWS. Which AWS program may support this company?
explanation:
APN Consulting Partners are professional services firms that help customers design, architect, build, migrate, and manage workloads on AWS. They provide architecture improvement services to AWS customers.

Other options:
- B. TAMs are AWS employees who support Enterprise customers, not partner companies.
- C. Technology Partners provide software products that run on AWS, not consulting services.
- D. Professional Services is an AWS team, not a program for external companies.

prompt: What is the AWS serverless service that allows you to run your applications without any administrative burden?
explanation:
AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code without provisioning or managing servers. You upload your code, and Lambda handles everything—scaling, patching, and capacity management automatically.

Other options:
- A. Lightsail provides virtual servers, not serverless compute.
- C. RDS instances are managed but not serverless; you still manage the database.
- D. EC2 instances require you to manage the operating system and server.

prompt: Jessica is managing an e-commerce web application in AWS. The application is hosted on six EC2 instances. One day, three of the instances crashed; but none of her customers were affected. What has Jessica done correctly in this scenario?

options.text: She has properly built an elastic system.
She has properly built a fault tolerant system.
She has properly built an encrypted system.
She has properly built a scalable system.

explanation:
A fault-tolerant system continues operating properly despite component failures. Jessica's architecture had redundancy so that when half the instances failed, the remaining instances handled the load without customer impact.

Other options:
- A. Elasticity refers to automatically scaling based on demand, not surviving failures.
- C. Encryption protects data but doesn't relate to surviving instance failures.
- D. Scalability is about handling growth; this scenario demonstrates fault tolerance.

prompt: Where can you store files in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Amazon EFS (Elastic File System) provides scalable file storage for use with EC2 instances. Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store) provides persistent block-level storage volumes for EC2 instances. Both are designed for file/data storage.

Other options:
- B. Amazon SNS is a notification service for pub/sub messaging, not file storage.
- D. Amazon ECS is a container orchestration service, not a storage service.
- E. Amazon EMR is a big data processing service using Hadoop/Spark, not storage.

prompt: Which AWS service can be used to store and reliably deliver messages across distributed systems?
explanation:
Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables decoupling and scaling of microservices and distributed systems. Messages are stored reliably until consumed.

Other options:
- B. Storage Gateway connects on-premises storage to cloud storage, not for messaging.
- C. SES is for sending emails, not for application-to-application messaging.
- D. S3 is object storage, not a messaging service.

prompt: Which of the following describes the payment model that AWS makes available for customers that can commit to using Amazon EC2 over a one or 3-year term to reduce their total computing costs?
explanation:
"Save when you reserve" describes Reserved Instances, where customers commit to 1 or 3-year terms in exchange for significant discounts (up to 72% compared to On-Demand). This is one of AWS's core pricing principles.

Other options:
- A. "Pay less as AWS grows" refers to AWS passing infrastructure savings to customers over time.
- B. "Pay as you go" describes On-Demand pricing with no commitment.
- C. "Pay less by using more" refers to tiered pricing and volume discounts.

prompt: A company is migrating its on-premises database to Amazon RDS. What should the company do to ensure Amazon RDS costs are kept to a minimum?

options.text: Combine On-demand Capacity Reservations with Saving Plans.

explanation:
Right-sizing means selecting the appropriate instance type and size based on actual workload requirements. Analyzing performance before and after migration helps avoid over-provisioning and reduces costs.

Other options:
- B. Multi-Region Active-Passive increases costs with redundant infrastructure.
- C. Capacity Reservations guarantee capacity but don't optimize costs for database workloads.
- D. Multi-Region Active-Active is the most expensive option, duplicating resources.

prompt: What is the primary storage service used by Amazon RDS database instances?
explanation:
Amazon RDS uses Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store) volumes for database and log storage. EBS provides the persistent, high-performance block storage that databases require for data integrity and performance.

Other options:
- A. Glacier is for archival storage with slow retrieval, unsuitable for active databases.
- C. EFS is file storage, not block storage needed for databases.
- D. S3 is object storage; RDS uses EBS for primary storage (though backups go to S3).

prompt: A company is developing a new application using a microservices framework. The new application is having performance and latency issues. Which AWS Service should be used to troubleshoot these issues?
explanation:
AWS X-Ray helps analyze and debug distributed applications, especially microservices. It provides end-to-end tracing, showing how requests travel through your application and identifying performance bottlenecks.

Other options:
- A. CodePipeline is for CI/CD automation, not application debugging.
- C. Inspector assesses security vulnerabilities, not performance issues.
- D. CloudTrail logs API calls for auditing, not application performance tracing.

prompt: Which of the following AWS services is designed with native Multi-AZ fault tolerance in mind? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Amazon S3 automatically stores data across multiple AZs within a region for 99.999999999% durability. DynamoDB automatically replicates data across multiple AZs, providing built-in high availability and fault tolerance.

Other options:
- A. Redshift is single-AZ by default; Multi-AZ requires additional configuration.
- B. Snowball is a physical data transfer device, not a service with AZ redundancy.
- D. EBS volumes exist in a single AZ; you must create snapshots for cross-AZ protection.

prompt: What are the Amazon RDS features that can be used to improve the availability of your database? (Choose TWO)
explanation:
Multi-AZ deployment provides a synchronous standby replica in a different AZ for automatic failover. Read Replicas improve availability by providing additional database copies that can be promoted to primary if needed.

Other options:
- A. Regions are geographic locations, not an RDS availability feature.
- C. Automatic patching is a maintenance feature, not an availability feature.
- E. Edge Locations are for CloudFront CDN, not database availability.

prompt: Sarah has deployed an application in the Northern California (us-west-1) region. After examining the application's traffic, she notices that about 30% of the traffic is coming from Asia. What can she do to reduce latency for the users in Asia?

options.text: Replicate the current resources across multiple Availability Zones within the same region.
Migrate the application to a hosting provider in Asia.
Create a CDN using CloudFront, so that content is cached at Edge Locations close to and in Asia.

explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is a CDN that caches content at edge locations worldwide, including many in Asia. This reduces latency by serving cached content from locations physically closer to Asian users.

Other options:
- A. Multiple AZs in us-west-1 won't reduce latency to Asia—it's still in California.
- B. Migrating away from AWS loses cloud benefits and isn't necessary.
- C. Recreating content doesn't address the geographic latency issue.

prompt: An organization runs many systems and uses many AWS products. Which of the following services enables them to control how each developer interacts with these products?
explanation:
AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management) enables you to manage access to AWS services and resources securely. You can create users, groups, and roles with specific permissions to control what each developer can do.

Other options:
- B. RDS is a database service, not for access management.
- C. NACLs control network traffic at the subnet level, not user permissions.
- D. EMR is for big data processing, not access control.

prompt: Using Amazon EC2 falls under which of the following cloud computing models?
explanation:
Amazon EC2 is Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS). You get virtual servers where you control the operating system, storage, and deployed applications. AWS manages the physical infrastructure while you manage everything from the OS up.

Other options:
- A. EC2 is purely IaaS, not combined with SaaS.
- C. SaaS provides complete applications; EC2 provides infrastructure to build on.
- D. PaaS (like Elastic Beanstalk) abstracts infrastructure; EC2 gives you direct control.

prompt: Which of the below is a best-practice when building applications on AWS?

options.text: Strengthen physical security by applying the principle of least privilege.
Ensure that the application runs on hardware from trusted vendors.
Use IAM policies to maintain performance.
Decouple the components of the application so that they run independently.

explanation:
Decoupling application components is a key AWS best practice. When components operate independently, failures in one component don't cascade to others, improving reliability and enabling independent scaling.

Other options:
- A. Least privilege is for access control, not physical security (which AWS handles).
- B. AWS manages hardware; customers don't choose vendors.
- C. IAM policies manage access permissions, not application performance.
